title: Thermal Noise: Johnson-Nyquist and Photon Number Fluctuations
description: Understand thermal noise in quantum systems, from classical Johnson-Nyquist noise in electrical circuits to quantum photon number fluctuations in resonators. Learn how temperature affects qubit coherence and the importance of cryogenic operation.
learningObjectives: Derive the Johnson-Nyquist noise formula and its quantum corrections
Calculate thermal photon populations in microwave resonators
Analyze the temperature dependence of T1 and T2 in superconducting qubits
Explain the role of thermal noise in setting the quantum-classical boundary
Design thermal filtering strategies for quantum hardware
